system: You are an ambitious AI PhD student focused on improving domain generalization across visually diverse real-world image domains using the DomainBed benchmark.
task_description:
You are working with DomainBed's ERM (Empirical Risk Minimization) method as the baseline on OfficeHome to evaluate generalization under real-world domain shifts. OfficeHome contains 65 categories of everyday objects across 4 visually distinct domains: Art (artistic renderings), Clipart (clipart style), Product (product photos without background), and Real World (real photos). The model is trained on 3 source domains and tested on 1 held-out target domain. Your goal is to improve out-of-distribution test accuracy on the held-out domain by developing better training algorithms, regularization techniques, domain alignment methods, or augmentation strategies.

Validation is measured on a held-out portion (30%) of the test domain (Art, env0). Test is measured on the remaining 70% of the test domain. Both measure out-of-distribution generalization to the same unseen domain, but on different data subsets. Use the validation results to guide your research, but be mindful that overfitting to the validation set will not improve test performance.

The training script runs with --algorithm ERM hardcoded, so the ERM class in algorithms.py is always used. You must modify the ERM class directly (or override its methods) rather than adding new algorithm classes — new classes will not be invoked. The algorithm class is obtained via algorithms.get_algorithm_class(algorithm_name) and instantiated with (input_shape, num_classes, num_domains, hparams). Do not change the algorithm constructor signature or the update() method signature.
